prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(register_pet_travel_case owner_profile_1)
(assign_document_source_to_case owner_profile_1 document_source_1)
(apply_regulatory_rule_to_case owner_profile_1 regulatory_rule_1)
(activate_case_after_rule_application owner_profile_1 regulatory_rule_1)
(assign_verifier_to_case owner_profile_1 verifier_role_1)
(verify_origin_requirement_for_owner owner_profile_1 origin_requirement_profile_1 regulatory_rule_1)
(confirm_owner_origin_verification owner_profile_1 origin_requirement_profile_1 verifier_role_1)
(release_verifier_from_case owner_profile_1 verifier_role_1)
(release_regulatory_rule_from_case owner_profile_1 regulatory_rule_1)
(register_pet_travel_case handler_profile_1)
(assign_document_source_to_case handler_profile_1 document_source_2)
(apply_regulatory_rule_to_case handler_profile_1 regulatory_rule_1)
(activate_case_after_rule_application handler_profile_1 regulatory_rule_1)
(assign_verifier_to_case handler_profile_1 verifier_role_1)
(verify_destination_requirement_for_handler handler_profile_1 destination_requirement_profile_1 regulatory_rule_1)
(confirm_handler_destination_verification handler_profile_1 destination_requirement_profile_1 verifier_role_1)
(release_verifier_from_case handler_profile_1 verifier_role_1)
(release_regulatory_rule_from_case handler_profile_1 regulatory_rule_1)
(create_assessment_from_verifications owner_profile_1 handler_profile_1 origin_requirement_profile_1 destination_requirement_profile_1 assessment_outcome_record_1)
(apply_regulatory_rule_to_case owner_profile_1 regulatory_rule_1)
(lock_assessment_for_review assessment_outcome_record_1 owner_profile_1 regulatory_rule_1)
(mark_owner_ready_for_final_check owner_profile_1 assessment_outcome_record_1)
(mark_handler_ready_for_final_check handler_profile_1 assessment_outcome_record_1)
(register_pet_travel_case application_record_1)
(release_regulatory_rule_from_case owner_profile_1 regulatory_rule_1)
(assign_document_source_to_case application_record_1 document_source_3)
(apply_regulatory_rule_to_case application_record_1 regulatory_rule_1)
(activate_case_after_rule_application application_record_1 regulatory_rule_1)
(link_authority_credential_to_application application_record_1 authority_credential_1)
(link_exception_to_application application_record_1 exception_category_1)
(consume_and_link_evidence_to_assessment application_record_1 evidence_item_1 assessment_outcome_record_1)
(advance_evaluator_stage_one application_record_1 evidence_item_1 assessment_outcome_record_1 regulatory_rule_1)
(assign_verifier_to_case application_record_1 verifier_role_1)
(advance_evaluator_stage_two_without_assessment_flag application_record_1 authority_credential_1 verifier_role_1 evidence_item_1 assessment_outcome_record_1)
(release_verifier_from_case application_record_1 verifier_role_1)
(unlink_authority_credential_from_application application_record_1 authority_credential_1)
(prepare_application_for_signoff application_record_1 exception_category_1 evidence_item_1 assessment_outcome_record_1)
(unlink_exception_from_application application_record_1 exception_category_1)
(mark_application_ready_for_final_check application_record_1)
(attach_notification_and_mark_pre_final_conditions application_record_1 notification_token_1 regulatory_rule_1)
(finalize_application_clearance_and_release_document_source application_record_1 document_source_3 notification_token_1)
(release_regulatory_rule_from_case application_record_1 regulatory_rule_1)
(register_pet_travel_case application_record_2)
(assign_document_source_to_case application_record_2 document_source_3)
(apply_regulatory_rule_to_case application_record_2 regulatory_rule_1)
(activate_case_after_rule_application application_record_2 regulatory_rule_1)
(link_authority_credential_to_application application_record_2 authority_credential_1)
(link_exception_to_application application_record_2 exception_category_1)
(consume_and_link_evidence_to_assessment application_record_2 evidence_item_2 assessment_outcome_record_1)
(advance_evaluator_stage_one application_record_2 evidence_item_2 assessment_outcome_record_1 regulatory_rule_1)
(assign_verifier_to_case application_record_2 verifier_role_1)
(advance_evaluator_stage_two_without_assessment_flag application_record_2 authority_credential_1 verifier_role_1 evidence_item_2 assessment_outcome_record_1)
(release_verifier_from_case application_record_2 verifier_role_1)
(unlink_authority_credential_from_application application_record_2 authority_credential_1)
(prepare_application_for_signoff application_record_2 exception_category_1 evidence_item_2 assessment_outcome_record_1)
(unlink_exception_from_application application_record_2 exception_category_1)
(mark_application_ready_for_final_check application_record_2)
(attach_notification_and_mark_pre_final_conditions application_record_2 notification_token_1 regulatory_rule_1)
(finalize_application_clearance_and_release_document_source application_record_2 document_source_3 notification_token_1)
(release_regulatory_rule_from_case application_record_2 regulatory_rule_1)
(apply_regulatory_rule_to_case owner_profile_1 regulatory_rule_1)
(attach_notification_and_mark_pre_final_conditions owner_profile_1 notification_token_1 regulatory_rule_1)
(finalize_owner_clearance_and_release_document_source owner_profile_1 document_source_1 notification_token_1)
(release_regulatory_rule_from_case owner_profile_1 regulatory_rule_1)
(apply_regulatory_rule_to_case handler_profile_1 regulatory_rule_1)
(attach_notification_and_mark_pre_final_conditions handler_profile_1 notification_token_1 regulatory_rule_1)
(finalize_handler_clearance_and_release_document_source handler_profile_1 document_source_2 notification_token_1)
message.reasoning:
The problem is a complex PDDL planning domain for cross-border pet travel policy. The goal is to clear owner_profile_1, handler_profile_1, application_record_1, and application_record_2 for travel.


## Investigating Travel Policies  
- The task focuses on a PDDL planning domain for cross-border pet travel.  
- The goal is to ensure clearance for owner_profile_1, handler_profile_1, and two application records for travel.

## Investigating Travel Policies  
- The goal requires cleared_for_travel for owner_profile_1, handler_profile_1, and application_records 1 and 2, but the predicate is typed for pet_travel_case.  
- There’s a type mismatch: case_party_group (including owner and handler profiles) and application_record_group are subtypes of pet_travel_case, explaining the usage.  

## Examining Type Hierarchy  
- The PDDL types show pet_travel_case as a subtype of case_family, with case_party_group and application_record_group also under pet_travel_case.  
- Syntax issues in type definitions suggest a need to clarify relationships, like document_source under administrative_resource.  

## Analyzing Available Resources  
- Special_condition_token_2 exists as an object but isn’t listed as available in init, unlike special_condition_token_1, linked to application_records.  
- No pet_travel_case instances are defined, despite predicates referencing them, indicating a potential setup issue.

## Examining Resource Availability  
- Two special condition tokens exist, but only special_condition_token_1 is available in init; token_2 is linked to app2 despite not being available.  
- Only one notification_token_1 is available, yet four items (owner1, handler1, app1, app2) need clearance, suggesting token reuse after release.  

## Assessing Shared Resources  
- Resources like verifier_role_1, regulatory_rule_1, and documents can be reused after release, supporting multiple clearance processes.  
- Special permits and fees (2 each) and single authority credential and exception category are available, indicating potential bottlenecks.  

## Exploring Clearance Process  
- Clearance for owner and handler profiles involves verifying origin/destination requirements, with paths using supporting documents or evidence.  
- Actions like verify_origin_requirement_for_owner and create_assessment_from_verifications suggest a sequence of verification steps, with special conditions affecting paths.

## Exploring Clearance Steps  
- Marking applications ready involves checking for special condition flags; without flags, it sets ready_for_signoff without credentials, allowing direct marking.  
- With flags, credentials_attached is set, requiring fee attachment and final evaluator review, ensuring owner and handler verifications are complete before final signoff.  

## Reviewing Special Conditions  
- Special condition tokens can be applied to active applications, setting them to awaiting status and consuming the token, followed by verifier verification.  
- Once verified, special conditions prepare for signoff, eventually leading to marked_complete and ready_for_final_check status.  

## Examining Assessment Creation  
- Assessments require owner and handler profiles to be combined and verified, with applications already linked to assessments in the initial setup.  
- Evidence must be consumed and linked to assessments to advance evaluator stages, with special permits needed if flag a is set on the assessment.

## Verifying Owner Profile  
- To verify owner_profile_1, first assign verifier_role_1, setting origin_verification_flag orig1.  
- After confirming, owner is marked combined and verified, allowing verifier release, but regulatory_rule_1 link may need to stay for locking assessment.  

## Processing Handler Profile  
- Handler_profile_1 follows a similar path: register, assign document_source_2, and apply regulatory_rule_1 after releasing it from owner.  
- Verification involves assigning verifier_role_1 (available after owner release), setting destination verification flag, and marking handler combined and verified.  

## Creating Assessment  
- With both owner and handler combined and verified, create_assessment_from_verifications links profiles and consumes a ticket, setting assessment created.  
- Locking assessment requires keeping regulatory_rule_1 linked to owner, avoiding early release to ensure record_rule_link for review.

## Managing Evidence and Rules  
- Consuming evidence for application_record_1 links it to assessment_outcome_record_1, setting evidence as consumed and unavailable after use.  
- Regulatory_rule_1 must remain linked to owner_profile_1 after locking the assessment to ensure rule availability for future steps.  

## Advancing Evaluator Stages  
- Advancing to stage two without flag b requires stage one completion, verifier assignment to the application, and evidence linked to assessment.  
- Preparing for signoff without special conditions sets the application ready_for_signoff, leading to marked_complete and ready_for_final_check if no credentials are attached.  

## Preparing for Final Clearance  
- Marking owner_profile_1 and handler_profile_1 ready for final check requires them to be combined, verified, and linked to a created, locked assessment.  
- Attaching notifications to profiles needs a linked rule and available notification token, which must be managed sequentially due to limited tokens.  

## Finalizing Clearance Process  
- Finalizing clearance for a profile requires it to be pre_final, with linked document reference and notification, setting it as cleared and releasing resources.  
- Document and notification links persist after finalization, allowing multiple finalizations with proper sequencing to manage limited resources.

## Linking Rules and Evidence  
- Apply regulatory_rule_1 to application_record_1, activating the case and linking authority credentials and exceptions.  
- Consume evidence_item_1, linking it to assessment_outcome_record_1, marking it as consumed and unavailable.  

## Advancing Evaluation Stages  
- Complete stage one for application_record_1, requiring active status, linked evidence, and regulatory rule.  
- Advance to stage two after assigning verifier_role_1, ensuring stage one completion and linked credentials, setting stage two complete.  

## Preparing for Signoff  
- Prepare application_record_1 for signoff without flags, requiring stage two completion and linked exceptions, marking it ready.  
- Since no credentials are attached, mark application_record_1 complete and ready for final check.  

## Managing Document Availability  
- Register application_record_2, but face document unavailability as all three documents (ds1, ds2, ds3) are assigned and not released.  
- Need to finalize clearance for some cases first to release documents, as attaching notifications requires ready_for_final_check and linked rules.

## Managing Resource Unlinking  
- After preparing application_record_1 for signoff, unlink exception_category_1 and authority_credential_1, making them available for other applications.  
- Unlinking occurs post-preparation but before marking complete, ensuring resources like documents and credentials can be reassigned.  

## Processing Application Sequence  
- For application_record_2, follow a similar sequence: register, assign available document ds3, and link authority credentials and exceptions.  
- Advance through stages, release verifier after stage two, and finalize by attaching notifications and releasing rules, clearing both applications.  

## Reapplying Rules for Profiles  
- After releasing regulatory_rule_1 from application_record_2, reapply it to owner_profile_1 for notification attachment.  
- Ensure owner_profile_1 is linked to a document and rule, then attach notification, setting it as pre_final and marking the notification as used.

## Listing Process Steps  
- Register pet travel cases for owner_profile_1 and handler_profile_1, linking each to respective document sources.  
- Apply regulatory_rule_1 to both profiles, activate cases, and assign verifiers for verification processes.  

## Verifying Requirements  
- Verify origin requirements for owner_profile_1 and destination requirements for handler_profile_1 using regulatory_rule_1.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
